steps.title: Identify what box plots reveal—and what they don't
steps.content: A box plot shows the median as the vertical line inside the box. It does NOT show the mean. Choices A and B claim facts about means, which cannot be determined from a box plot alone. Only C and D make claims about medians, which are directly visible. We can immediately eliminate A and B.

steps.title: Read the median from each box plot
steps.content: The median is the line dividing the box. For Group 1, this line falls at 24 kg. For Group 2, this line falls at approximately 23 kg (positioned between the box edges that span from about 23 to 26). Group 1's median is clearly positioned to the right of Group 2's median on the scale.

steps.title: Compare the medians and select the correct choice
steps.content: Group 1's median (24 kg) is greater than Group 2's median (~23 kg). That matches choice C — C is correct.
